title: Operations and Support
subtitle: A compact starting shape for mediated workflows, support surfaces, and the tools that keep them operational.
scope: A support or operations domain where requests, operational surfaces, enablers, and tooling need to be reasoned about together.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace placeholder workflows, code paths, tickets, and tooling references with real operational sources.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when requests, support lanes, enablers, and operator tooling all need to be visible in one operational view.
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Treat support as an operational system with intake, mediation, operator work, enablers, and tools visible as separate but connected surfaces.
keepDistinct: tooling used to operate the domain

semanticDefinition.objectiveRole: Make an operations or support domain legible across request flow, operating surfaces, enabling assets, and tooling.
semanticDefinition.templateSubtitle: A compact starting shape for mediated workflows, support surfaces, and the tools that keep them operational.
semanticDefinition.templateScope: A support or operations domain where requests, operational surfaces, enablers, and tooling need to be reasoned about together.
semanticDefinition.templateObjective: Make an operations or support domain legible across request flow, operational surfaces, enabling assets, and the tools used to support the work.
semanticDefinition.templateSuccessCondition: A reader can see how requests enter the system, which support paths exist, what enablers are available, and which tools the operators rely on.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.role: Summarize operational readiness and current support pressure without carrying detailed request or tooling evidence.
sections.rationale: This section keeps operational readiness visible without turning requests, support lanes, enablers, or tooling into dashboard prose.

sections.title: Mediated Operation
sections.role: Describe the mediated request-to-execution flow that operators need to run.
sections.rationale: The core operation has to name how work enters, moves, and gets mediated before support surfaces can be evaluated.

sections.title: Operating Surface
sections.role: Expose the bounded support lanes or operational paths that request flow routes into.
sections.rationale: Support lanes are separated from request flow so each bounded operating path can be assessed and improved.

sections.title: Enabler Catalog
sections.role: Catalog compact support assets that improve operator speed, reliability, or coverage.
sections.rationale: Operators need visible snippets, assets, fixtures, or runbooks that make the operating surfaces easier and safer to run.

sections.title: Tooling Surface
sections.role: Expose the scripts, workflows, and local tools used to operate or evolve the support domain.
sections.rationale: Operational claims need concrete tool paths so support work can be rerun, inspected, or extended.

enabler-catalog.name: Enabler Catalog
enabler-catalog.description: A dense catalog of enabling, support, remediation, or auxiliary work items that sit around the core flow.
enabler-catalog.structuralContract: Three-column card grid of block-status items. Use for compact inventories of enabling assets or interventions where breadth matters more than narrative flow.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat the section as a breadth-first catalog of enabling or auxiliary work around the core flow.
inspect: Check whether each item enables the core flow rather than being the core flow.
update: Keep entries compact and catalog-like so breadth remains visible.
avoid: Do not use this for single-lane operational narratives or primary component surfaces.

enabler-catalog.icon: <rect x='4' y='4' width='5' height='5' rx='1.2' opacity='.3'/><rect x='10.5' y='4' width='5' height='5' rx='1.2' opacity='.5'/><rect x='17' y='4' width='3' height='5' rx='1.2' opacity='.75'/><rect x='4' y='11' width='5' height='5' rx='1.2' opacity='.5'/><rect x='10.5' y='11' width='5' height='5' rx='1.2' opacity='.75'/><path d='M17 12h3v3h-3zm0 4.5h3V20h-3z'/>

operating-surface.name: Operating Surface
operating-surface.description: A bounded operational or implementation surface described as a single narrative lane of work items.
operating-surface.structuralContract: One-column card grid of block-status items. Use when the section is best read as one coherent surface, boundary, side channel, or support area rather than parallel component columns.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat the section as one coherent operational or implementation lane.
inspect: Read the lane as a whole before changing individual item status.
update: Preserve one-column narrative flow and avoid splitting the surface into parallel inventory cards.
avoid: Do not use this for broad component inventories or formal model claims.

operating-surface.icon: <rect x='4' y='5' width='16' height='14' rx='2' opacity='.22'/><path d='M7 9h10v2H7zm0 4h6v2H7z'/><circle cx='17' cy='14' r='2.5'/>

operation.name: Mediated Operation
operation.description: An operator-mediated workflow surface where human or agent routing turns a request into coordinated execution.
operation.structuralContract: Two-column card grid of probe-status items focused on workflow support, mediation, and next-step readiness.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat the section as a mediated workflow where a request is routed into coordinated execution.
inspect: Check the current request path and operator or agent support before updating readiness.
update: Keep mediation, support state, and gaps visible as separate fields.
avoid: Do not convert this into general capability status or verification evidence.

status-snapshot.name: Status Snapshot
status-snapshot.description: A summary section that compresses the current document into top-level counts and distribution signals.
status-snapshot.structuralContract: Stats-first section with snapshot cards and no item grid requirement. Use for high-level totals and state distribution, not for individual entities.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat the section as document-wide compression, not as an item inventory.
inspect: Read the rest of the document before changing snapshot stats.
update: Recompute stats and callout language from the current section set and payload.
avoid: Do not add individual work items here.

status-snapshot.icon: <rect x='4' y='14' width='3' height='6' rx='1' opacity='.4'/><rect x='10.5' y='9' width='3' height='11' rx='1' opacity='.65'/><rect x='17' y='5' width='3' height='15' rx='1'/>

tooling-surface.name: Tooling Surface
tooling-surface.description: An operational tooling surface that collects the skills, scripts, workflows, and local instruments used to inspect, verify, or modify the domain without treating them as source material.
tooling-surface.structuralContract: Two-column card grid of tooling-status items. Each item should represent one operational instrument with clear purpose, invocation, and caveats. Use when the section is about how the domain is worked on rather than what the domain says.

promptGuidance.operatingThesis: Treat the section as the operational instrument layer used to inspect, verify, modify, or refresh the domain.
inspect: Check whether each tool is currently trusted, caveated, prototype, broken, or missing.
update: Keep purpose, when-to-use, invocation, and caveats explicit for each tool.
avoid: Do not treat tooling entries as primary source material or domain claims.

relationships.rationale: Operational status should summarize real support flow rather than stand apart as a dashboard label.
Mediated operations should name the concrete support lanes or operating surfaces they route requests into.

evidence.description: An operating surface should identify the operation, workflow path, or request input it supports.

relationships.rationale: Support lanes stay operational only when their enabling snippets, fixtures, runbooks, or assets are visible.
Operational request flow needs a tool path so a future session can rerun, inspect, or extend the support process.

stageSignals.question: What first real request flow, intake path, or mediated operation makes this support doc tangible?

stageSignals.when: operation exists but operating-surface has no corresponding support lanes
stageSignals.question: Which operation cards lack a concrete support lane or operating surface?

stageSignals.when: operating-surface has lanes but enabler-catalog has no assets that support them
stageSignals.question: Which enabler would make the current support lane faster, safer, or easier to operate?

stageSignals.when: operation, operating surface, enablers, and tooling are all populated and linked
stageSignals.question: Can the support domain be operated from this document, or are key request paths still implicit?

validOperations.label: Add operation card
validOperations.description: Add a request-flow or mediated-operation card that names how work enters and moves through the support domain.

validOperations.label: Add operating surface card
validOperations.description: Add a bounded support lane or operational surface that a request flow routes into.

validOperations.label: Add enabler card
validOperations.description: Add a support asset, runbook, fixture, or snippet that strengthens an operating surface.

validOperations.label: Link tooling to operation
validOperations.description: Link the script, workflow, or local tool used to run or inspect the operation.

validOperations.label: Lower support status
validOperations.description: Lower or qualify operational readiness when support lanes, enablers, or tooling do not support the claim.

objective: Make an operations or support domain legible across request flow, operational surfaces, enabling assets, and the tools used to support the work.
successCondition: A reader can see how requests enter the system, which support paths exist, what enablers are available, and which tools the operators rely on.

callouts.title: How to use this template
items: Use this for support and mediation domains where routing, readiness, and support tooling matter more than design alignment.
Keep the operation section focused on request flow and the tooling section focused on the instruments used to run the domain.

sections.title: Status Snapshot

callout.title: Template intent
items: Use the snapshot to show how much of the support surface is actually ready versus merely planned.
Let the following sections carry the operational detail instead of overloading the top of the document.

sections.rationale: This section keeps operational readiness visible without turning requests, support lanes, enablers, or tooling into dashboard prose.

sections.title: Mediated Operation

data.name: Incoming request intake
data.currentSupport: Operators can accept structured requests and route them into the right support path.
data.nextStep: Add tighter validation for malformed or underspecified requests.
notes: The operation section is for request-to-execution flow, not for general background notes.
gaps: No automated classification for ambiguous inputs yet.

data.name: Fulfillment routing
data.currentSupport: Requests can be routed manually into known execution paths.
data.nextStep: Define stronger handoff rules for blocked or multi-step cases.
gaps: Escalation paths still depend on operator memory.

sections.rationale: The core operation has to name how work enters, moves, and gets mediated before support surfaces can be evaluated.

sections.title: Operating Surface

data.name: Account reset path
notes: Use the operating surface for the bounded support lanes the operators actually run.

data.name: Purchase seeding path
notes: Keep missing support paths visible instead of letting them live only in backlog text.

sections.rationale: Support lanes are separated from request flow so each bounded operating path can be assessed and improved.

sections.title: Enabler Catalog

data.name: Operator FAQ snippets
notes: Use enabler sections for compact support assets that strengthen the main operating lanes.

data.name: Seed data fixtures
notes: Enablers are not the main workflow, but they materially improve support speed and reliability.

data.name: Escalation runbook notes
notes: Missing enablers should stay visible if they repeatedly slow down operator work.

sections.rationale: Operators need visible snippets, assets, fixtures, or runbooks that make the operating surfaces easier and safer to run.

sections.title: Tooling Surface

data.name: Support router
data.purpose: Normalize incoming support requests and route them into the right operational lane.
data.whenToUse: Use when operator demand is high enough that manual routing becomes the bottleneck.
notes: Replace placeholder routing assets with the real support tools in your domain.
caveats: Prototype routing logic should not be treated as an authority layer until its failure modes are understood.

data.name: Living doc compositor
data.purpose: Capture the support surface as a shareable operational snapshot without losing the structure needed for later updates.
data.whenToUse: Use when support knowledge needs to be distributed beyond the operators who currently hold it in memory.
caveats: The tool packages the snapshot well, but it does not remove the need to keep the canonical JSON current.

sections.rationale: Operational claims need concrete tool paths so support work can be rerun, inspected, or extended.
